symptoms.symptom_name: Beefy-red, weeping, macerated erythema confined to the inguinal and subpannicular skin folds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confluent beefy-red maceration in the depths of warm moist folds is the hallmark distribution and appearance of candidal intertrigo.
Erythrasma is a mild brown-red finely-scaling eruption, not an acute beefy-red weeping one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Satellite papules and pustules beyond a sharply-defined border

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Satellite pustules outside the main plaque are the near-defining feature distinguishing candidal intertrigo from tinea and inverse psoriasis.
Tinea shows an active scaly advancing border with central clearing rather than satellite pustules.
Inverse psoriasis is smooth and non-pustular; satellite pustules argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent 10-day broad-spectrum antibiotic course on a background of poorly-controlled type 2 diabetes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Antibiotic disruption of competing flora plus a hyperglycaemic substrate are the two classic precipitants of cutaneous candidal overgrowth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no spreading induration/heat beyond the folds, no purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic and spreading features makes an established secondary cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target in a diabetic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe friction pain with temporary loss of mobility in a warm humid environment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe fold pain, occlusion and a warm humid environment fit a severe intertrigo and drive the symptom-relief and drying advice; non-specific for the exact organism.

symptoms.symptom_name: No oropharyngeal white plaques and no other mucosal involvement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the process is confined to the skin (cutaneous) rather than a mucocutaneous or disseminated candidiasis; does not change the local diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals and weight (BP 140/85, HR 92, Temp 37.0, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Wt 110 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; afebrile and haemodynamically stable argues against systemic infection. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prompt in-person GP examination of the skin folds to exclude early secondary cellulitis and confirm the diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The one assessment telehealth cannot do — palpation for heat/induration/tenderness and direct inspection of the deep folds in a poorly-controlled diabetic with broken skin.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood glucose / HbA1c review to characterise and optimise diabetic control
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Poor glycaemic control is feeding the candidal overgrowth and impairing healing; optimising it is part of definitive management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping / swab for fungal microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms candida versus a dermatophyte and guides antifungal choice if the response is poor; not required before starting empirical topical treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for bacterial microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — no fever, spreading erythema or purulence. Reserve for if secondary bacterial infection develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Systemic bloods / septic screen / blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No systemic features; ordering these now would be over-investigation of a localised skin condition.
